channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Construction Simulator: Evolution Ratchets Up the Destruction This Year
item.content:encoded:
Astragon Entertainment and the snappily-named Weltenbauer Software Entwicklung have announced Construction Simulator: Evolution, a brand new entry in the Construction Simulator series, and it sounds like the perfect outlet for your more destructive tendencies.

Construction Simulator: Evolution is due to land on PC and consoles later this year, the two studios revealed during today's Galaxies Spring Showcase, and they're promising that the new game, which follows on from 2022's previous franchise entry, will offer "the most immersive construction worker experience so far".

According to Astragon and Weltenbauer, Construction Simulator: Evolution represents "the next evolutionary stage" of the series (hence the name, I suppose). It's packing tons of new vehicles across 35 well-known real-life brands, including new partners like Volvo, Epiroc, and Sennebogen.

As well as new vehicles, Construction Simulator: Evolution will also feature a brand new destruction mechanic, so not only can you build things, but you can also knock them down and laugh amidst the ruins (well, that might just be me).

Excitingly, Construction Simulator: Evolution also adds manual labor mechanics and tools, so you can now use "hydraulic hammers, table saws, nailguns", and other tools to complete on-the-ground work.

You'll find two brand new maps in Evolution, one inspired by Germany and the other by the US. Each map has its own campaign, too, both of which will prove "challenging", Astragon and Weltenbauer say.

Combine all of that with multiplayer support, as well as support for peripherals like steering wheels, and you've got what the two studios hope will be the best version of Construction Simulator to date.

Right now, the plan is to release Construction Simulator: Evolution on PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S in late 2026, although we don't have a more specific release date than that yet, unfortunately. In the meantime, maybe go back and check out the 2022 version if you're in a construction mood.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/construction-simulator-evolution-announced
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Avalon Hill Announces Three New Board Games Coming to Gen Con, Bringing Revolution and Reviving Gothic Horror
item.content:encoded:
Board game publisher Avalon Hill has new board game projects coming to this year's Gen Con. The studio well known for titles like Heroscape and Betrayal at House on the Hill will bring a horror-themed expansion for Talisman, and a revival of a fan favorite.

In an official press release, Avalon Hill announced three board games will be coming to Gen Con 2026. The first of these, Guillotine, is a party game all about glorious revolution. Players take turns "operating" a guillotine set to execute a noble, drawn from a random deck of cards. Players then spend action cards to manipulate the line in order to execute the noble with the most points.

It's a shorter card game experience meant to be finished in 20 minutes. If you are unable to attend Gen Con this year, Guillotine will be available at retail stores on August 1, 2026 at an MSRP of $19.99.

The second title is an expansion for the adventure board game, Talisman. Talisman Sagas: Crown of Blood combines the original gameplay with the gothic horror of Bram Stoker's Dracula. Players choose between playing as vampire hunters Mina Harker or Professor Van Helsing as they face enemies and obtain magical objects, all in service of their ultimate goal of slaying Count Dracula.

Talisman Sagas: Crown of Blood will be available June 15, 2026, about six months before the start of Gen Con 2026. The game will be sold at an MSRP of $19.99

The third Avalon Hill game is a brand new edition of Fury of Dracula, another title inspired by Bram Stoker's novel. This new edition continues the game's blend of gothic horror and social deduction. A group of players take the role of vampire hunters as they try to stop Dracula's rampage through Europe. Another player is the Count in disguise, feeding and killing in secret. 

Fury of Dracula Fifth Edition will be available for purchase September 1, 2026 at an MSRP of $59.99.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/avalon-hill-announces-three-new-board-games-coming-to-gen-con-bringing-revolution-and
